# PrintMoneyLab Tools

> Free interactive tools for the LA 2028 Summer Olympics. Built for fans planning to watch, travel to, or follow the Games.

This site provides timezone conversion, schedule browsing, and ticket cost tools for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympic Games.

## Tools

### LA 2028 Schedule in Your Timezone
- URL: https://tools.printmoneylab.com/la28-schedule-your-timezone/
- Data: 772 competition sessions, 36 sports, 223 medal sessions
- Source: LA28 Official Competition Schedule v2.1 (February 6, 2025)
- Features: Auto timezone detection, filter by sport/date/medals, 40+ city timezones
- Schedule data file: https://tools.printmoneylab.com/la28-schedule-your-timezone/schedule.json

## Key Facts (LA 2028 Olympics)

- Opening Ceremony: Friday, July 14, 2028 (SoFi Stadium + LA Memorial Coliseum)
- Closing Ceremony: Sunday, July 30, 2028 (LA Memorial Coliseum)
- Competition dates: July 12-30, 2028 (19 days)
- Total sessions: 772
- Medal sessions: 223
- Sports: 36 (including 5 new/returning: baseball/softball, cricket T20, flag football, lacrosse sixes, squash)
- Primary timezone: Pacific Time (PT / UTC-7 in July)
- Oklahoma City events (softball, canoe slalom): Central Time (CT / UTC-5), 26 sessions
- Ticket service fee: 24% on face value (confirmed by LA28 and multiple news outlets)

## Timezone Quick Reference (for July 2028)

- Los Angeles (PT): UTC-7 (base timezone for all events except OKC)
- New York (ET): UTC-4 (PT + 3 hours)
- London (BST): UTC+1 (PT + 8 hours)
- Paris/Berlin (CEST): UTC+2 (PT + 9 hours)
- Dubai (GST): UTC+4 (PT + 11 hours)
- Mumbai (IST): UTC+5:30 (PT + 12.5 hours)
- Seoul/Tokyo (KST/JST): UTC+9 (PT + 16 hours)
- Sydney (AEST): UTC+10 (PT + 17 hours)

## Data Source

Schedule data: LA28 Olympic Games Competition Schedule by Event, Version 2.1
Published: February 6, 2025
Publisher: LA28 Organizing Committee
Available at: https://la28.org/en/competition-schedule

## About

Publisher: PrintMoneyLab (https://printmoneylab.com)
This is an independent project. Not affiliated with LA28, the IOC, or any official Olympic body.
Contact: contact@printmoneylab.com
